advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an MCP test that sign_review under REBAR_MCP_READONLY=1 raises (is refused), and that a writable server passes through to resign_plan_review.
evidence: src/rebar/_mcp_llm.py: `if _readonly(): raise ValueError("sign_review is disabled: it writes a SIGNATURE event (readonly mode).")`

ABSENCE: `search_files 'sign_review' tests` finds only the docstring entry in test_mcp_output_schema_coverage.py:117 and the CLI test name — no `srv.call_tool("sign_review", ...)` and no REBAR_MCP_READONLY test for it.

Peer write-guards ARE tested: tests/interfaces/facades/test_mcp.py:256 test_fsck_recover_blocked_under_readonly and :214 test_reconcile_mutating_refused_under_readonly assert the raise under readonly.
advisory.finding: The new MCP `sign_review` tool's REBAR_MCP_READONLY write-guard (raises ValueError because it writes a SIGNATURE event) has no test — no test invokes the MCP tool at all; the schema-coverage change only classifies it. A regression that inverted/dropped the guard would let a read-only server persist a signature undetected.
scenarios: A refactor of _readonly()/the guard clause drops or inverts the readonly check for sign_review; a readonly MCP server then writes a SIGNATURE event and no test fails.
advisory.suggested_fix: In tests/interfaces/facades/test_mcp.py, add a test that sets REBAR_MCP_READONLY=1, builds the server, and asserts srv.call_tool('sign_review', {...}) raises with 'readonly'/'SIGNATURE' in the message; plus a writable control that reaches resign_plan_review.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test where the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar records verdict=PASS with coverage.resolution_class set, asserting resign_plan_review refuses (ok=False, reason names resolution_class) and writes no signature.
evidence: This mirrors the structural never-sign guard at src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py:303 which explicitly refuses a PASS carrying resolution_class.

ABSENCE: test_resign_refuses_when_no_pass_sidecar only exercises a BLOCK sidecar (verdict!=PASS); no test constructs a PASS sidecar with coverage.resolution_class set to hit the degraded-PASS refusal branch (resign.py:76-79 emits the resolution_class-specific reason).
advisory.finding: resign_plan_review re-implements the never-sign guard `verdict!=PASS OR coverage.resolution_class`, but the refusal test only covers the verdict!=PASS half (a BLOCK sidecar). The degraded-PASS half (verdict==PASS with a truthy coverage.resolution_class) is never tested, so dropping that clause would silently re-sign a degraded PASS.
scenarios: A PASS review that is systemically degraded (resolution_class set) is left unsigned; the resign path is asked to recover it. If the resolution_class clause were removed, resign would sign a degraded PASS and the gate would accept an uncertifiable result — no test catches it.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] resign_plan_review binds CURRENT code/regver but only guards on material fingerprint — carry forward (and re-verify) the review-time code SHA and registry version, or refuse when they cannot be proven unchanged, so a re-sign cannot certify code/criteria the review never examined.
evidence: resign.py:86-89 staleness guard only recomputes material: `recorded_material = payload.get("material_fingerprint")` / `current_material = attest.current_material_fingerprint(...)` — no code-SHA or regver comparison
resign.py:113-116 comment concedes re-baselining: `dependency scoping falls to the ticket's current file_impact ... hashed at the current code — the recovery attestation binds current code`

attest.sign_plan_review re-stamps current axes each call: attest.py:330 `regver=registry_version(repo_root)` and attest.py:331 `verified_at_sha=current_code_sha()`, deps hashed at current code (attest.py:315)

claim gate enforces all three axes on read: attest.py:637 `signed_regver != registry_version(repo_root)` → stale-regver; attest.py:653-665 per-path dep drift → stale-code; attest.py:689 material → stale-material
material_fingerprint hashes ticket material only, NOT code content: pass1.py:555-560 basis = {ticket_id, description, file_impact, children}

REVIEW_RESULT sidecar payload does NOT persist verified_at_sha or regver (build_payload sidecar.py:360-378; search for `verified_at_sha`/`regver` in sidecar.py returned no matches), so resign has no review-time code/registry baseline to carry forward even if it wanted to
the legitimate cheap-reuse path drift_refresh_candidate REQUIRES regver+material unchanged and probes before refreshing on code drift (attest.py:382-395), whereas resign re-binds unconditionally
advisory.finding: resign_plan_review's staleness guard checks only the material fingerprint (ticket description/AC/file_impact/children) and re-binds CURRENT code and CURRENT criteria-registry version, so it can produce a valid plan-review attestation that certifies code and/or criteria the LLM review never examined — bypassing two of the three invalidation axes the claim gate normally enforces (stale-code and stale-regver).
scenarios: A plan-review computes PASS but the SIGN commit fails; the code and/or .rebar/criteria_routing.json overlay drift afterward; later `rebar sign-review <id>` (ticket material byte-identical) signs an attestation binding the drifted code/criteria, and `rebar claim` then passes the gate against code the review never saw

An operator disables/activates a project criterion (changing registry_version) after a review, then runs sign-review: the recovered attestation stamps the NEW regver, so the claim gate's stale-regver check that would have forced a re-review is defeated
advisory.suggested_fix: Persist the review-time verified_at_sha and regver in the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar (build_payload), and in resign_plan_review require current_code_sha()==recorded_sha and registry_version(repo_root)==recorded_regver (in addition to material) before signing; otherwise refuse with a 'code/criteria drifted since review — run review-plan' reason, mirroring drift_refresh_candidate's guards.
